passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: Not only does regular exercise improve physical health, ----.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text: Although the novel was written over a century ago, ----.

questions.question: Although most species of sharks are not inherently aggressive toward humans, ----.

options.A: they are often portrayed as mindless killers in popular cinema
options.B: the ocean provides a vast habitat for diverse marine life
options.C: marine biologists have spent decades studying their migratory patterns
options.D: only a few species are responsible for the majority of unprovoked attacks
options.E: conservation efforts have successfully increased their population in the Pacific

questions.question: Scientists are increasingly concerned about the melting of the polar ice caps ----.

options.A: because it contributes significantly to the rising of global sea levels
options.B: since the local ecosystem is surprisingly resilient to temperature shifts
options.C: even if the data suggests a temporary cooling in some regions
options.D: unless the industrial nations agree to reduce carbon emissions
options.E: so that the path for international shipping remains open all year

questions.question: While the economic downturn has impacted many sectors severely, ----.

options.A: the unemployment rate is expected to reach record highs by next year
options.B: some technology firms have actually seen a boost in their digital sales
options.C: government subsidies were insufficient to prevent many small businesses from closing
options.D: investors are hesitant to commit capital to high-risk ventures
options.E: the global supply chain has faced unprecedented logistical challenges

questions.question: ---- so that they can provide more accurate weather forecasts for the upcoming decade.

options.A: Meteorologists are integrating advanced satellite data with AI algorithms
options.B: The historical climate data has been misinterpreted by many researchers
options.C: Atmospheric pressure varies significantly across different geographical zones
options.D: Natural disasters have become more frequent due to human-induced climate change
options.E: The ozone layer is gradually recovering from past chemical damage

questions.question: In spite of the widespread availability of digital books, ----.

options.A: traditional libraries continue to be valued as community hubs and study spaces
options.B: the publishing industry has shifted its focus entirely to electronic formats
options.C: most young readers prefer the convenience of using tablets and e-readers
options.D: authors are finding it difficult to protect their intellectual property online
options.E: paper shortages have led to a significant increase in the price of hardcovers

questions.question: ---- even though the initial results of the clinical trial appeared promising.

options.A: The medical board decided to halt the study due to unexpected side effects
options.B: Patients reported a significant improvement in their physical health
options.C: The new drug was approved for mass distribution within six months
options.D: Researchers published their findings in a prestigious medical journal
options.E: Further funding was secured to expand the research to other hospitals

questions.question: Since the invention of the printing press in the 15th century, ----.

options.A: the dissemination of knowledge has become exponentially faster and more accessible
options.B: scribes were no longer required to copy manuscripts by hand in monasteries
options.C: illiteracy was a common problem among the lower classes of society
options.D: Gutenberg is remembered as one of the most influential figures in history
options.E: books were once considered luxury items reserved only for the elite

questions.question: Just as the Industrial Revolution transformed the structure of urban life, ----.

options.A: the Digital Age is currently reshaping how we interact and work
options.B: many people migrated from rural areas to find employment in factories
options.C: it led to significant advancements in steam engine technology and transport
options.D: labor unions were formed to protect the rights of the working class
options.E: environmental degradation became a major concern for future generations

questions.question: Unless sustainable agricultural practices are adopted worldwide, ----.

options.A: the global food supply will face a critical threat from soil degradation
options.B: farmers will continue to rely on traditional methods of cultivation
options.C: the population growth rate is expected to stabilize by the end of the century
options.D: technological innovations have improved crop yields in many regions
options.E: organic produce has become more popular among health-conscious consumers

questions.question: Despite the fact that renewable energy sources are becoming more cost-effective, ----.

options.A: many countries still rely heavily on fossil fuels for their energy needs
options.B: the installation of solar panels has increased by forty percent this year
options.C: wind turbines are now a common sight in many coastal landscapes
options.D: the transition to green energy is essential for a sustainable future
options.E: investments in hydroelectric power have yielded high returns for investors

questions.question: Because the human brain is highly neuroplastic, ----.

options.A: it can reorganize itself by forming new neural connections throughout life
options.B: it is often compared to a complex computer processing information
options.C: it requires a constant supply of oxygen and glucose to function
options.D: certain areas are responsible for specific functions like speech and motor skills
options.E: it reaches its full developmental potential by the age of twenty-five

questions.question: Archaeologists have discovered ancient ruins that suggest ----.

options.A: the civilization had a much more sophisticated irrigation system than previously thought
options.B: the site was abandoned after a massive volcanic eruption occurred
options.C: they are looking for more artifacts to prove their hypothesis
options.D: local residents were unaware of the historical significance of the area
options.E: the excavation process will take several more years to complete

questions.question: ---- if the central bank decides to lower the interest rates in the next quarter.

options.A: Borrowing costs for businesses and consumers will likely decrease
options.B: The inflation rate had already reached its peak during the winter
options.C: Economic growth is often measured by the Gross Domestic Product
options.D: Many investors prefer to keep their assets in gold and other commodities
options.E: The government has announced a new fiscal policy to reduce debt

questions.question: Whereas the internal combustion engine relies on fossil fuels, ----.

options.A: electric vehicles utilize rechargeable batteries to power their motors
options.B: hybrid cars are becoming increasingly popular among urban drivers
options.C: it produces greenhouse gases that contribute to global warming
options.D: petroleum is a finite resource that is being depleted rapidly
options.E: engineers are working on improving the efficiency of solar panels

questions.question: The primary goal of the new educational reform is to ensure ----.

options.A: that every student has equal access to high-quality learning resources
options.B: the curriculum was updated to include more extracurricular activities
options.C: teachers are provided with additional training and professional development
options.D: school facilities are renovated to meet modern safety standards
options.E: standardized tests are the only measure of academic success

questions.question: ---- because the original hypothesis failed to account for environmental variables.

options.A: The researchers had to revise their experimental design and methodology
options.B: The study was published in several peer-reviewed scientific journals
options.C: The data collected during the first phase of the trial was very consistent
options.D: Many scholars agreed that the findings were groundbreaking and innovative
options.E: The lab equipment was upgraded to ensure more precise measurements

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: but it also plays a crucial role in enhancing mental well-being
options.B: and it is recommended for people of all age groups and backgrounds
options.C: so that individuals can maintain a healthy weight and lifestyle
options.D: since it reduces the risk of developing chronic diseases such as diabetes
options.E: it is often overlooked by those with busy work schedules

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Several diplomatic hurdles had to be overcome through intense negotiations
options.B: The agreement aims to reduce carbon emissions by thirty percent
options.C: International law governs the relations between different nations
options.D: The signing ceremony was held in the capital city last month
options.E: Trade barriers have been removed to facilitate economic cooperation

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: more than two-thirds of the world population will live in cities by 2050
options.B: rural areas are losing their young population to metropolitan centers
options.C: infrastructure in large cities is already struggling to cope with the demand
options.D: the government should invest more in public transportation systems
options.E: urban planning has become a critical field for sustainable development

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: while proponents claim it will stimulate economic growth by rewarding investment
options.B: the government has yet to release the full details of the legislation
options.C: many citizens have expressed their concerns through public protests
options.D: it was designed to close loopholes used by large corporations
options.E: the finance minister defended the bill during a heated debate in parliament

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The company immediately shut down its servers to prevent data theft
options.B: Cybersecurity has become a top priority for businesses worldwide
options.C: Hackers often use sophisticated methods to bypass encryption
options.D: The software update was designed to fix several known vulnerabilities
options.E: Users were advised to change their passwords every three months

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: its themes of social injustice and human greed remain remarkably relevant
options.B: it has been translated into more than fifty different languages
options.C: the author was a well-known figure in the literary circles of the time
options.D: it tells the story of a young man struggling to find his place in society
options.E: many modern readers find the language and style difficult to understand

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Tourism activities are strictly regulated by the local authorities
options.B: The island is home to several endemic species of plants and animals
options.C: Climate change poses a significant threat to low-lying coastal areas
options.D: Many researchers have visited the island to study its biodiversity
options.E: The government plans to build a new luxury resort on the coast

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: the precise coordination between ground control and the astronauts
options.B: the development of new propulsion systems and fuel technologies
options.C: the mission was delayed several times due to technical difficulties
options.D: many international partners contributed to the funding and expertise
options.E: the public's interest in space exploration has increased recently

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Psychologists are conducting experiments in simulated space habitats
options.B: The study of human behavior has evolved significantly over the years
options.C: Mental health awareness has become a major issue in modern society
options.D: Solitary confinement is often used as a form of punishment in prisons
options.E: Social interaction is essential for the emotional development of children

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: it still maintains its headquarters in the small town where it was founded
options.B: its profits have reached record levels during the last fiscal year
options.C: it has faced stiff competition from local businesses in many markets
options.D: the management decided to hire more staff to handle the increased workload
options.E: it offers a wide range of products tailored to different cultural preferences

questions.question: The city council has implemented a new recycling program ----.

options.A: to reduce the amount of waste that ends up in local landfills
options.B: which was initially met with skepticism by many residents
options.C: because environmental sustainability is a key priority for the administration
options.D: since the old system was inefficient and costly to maintain
options.E: as more people are becoming aware of the importance of recycling

questions.question: ---- just as the human heart pumps blood to all parts of the body.

options.A: The engine serves as the core component that distributes power to the machine
options.B: The nervous system transmits signals between the brain and the limbs
options.C: The sun provides energy that sustains all forms of life on Earth
options.D: The central bank regulates the flow of money within the economy
options.E: The root system absorbs water and nutrients from the soil for the plant

questions.question: Despite being one of the most prolific composers of his time, ----.

options.A: Mozart struggled with financial difficulties throughout much of his life
options.B: his music is still performed in concert halls all over the world today
options.C: he began composing at a very young age under his father's guidance
options.D: his works include symphonies, operas, and chamber music
options.E: he was highly respected by his contemporaries and the royal court

questions.question: Researchers have found a correlation between sleep deprivation and ----.

options.A: an increased risk of developing various chronic health conditions
options.B: the amount of caffeine consumed by individuals during the day
options.C: the quality of dreams experienced during the REM cycle
options.D: the number of hours people spend working in high-stress jobs
options.E: the use of electronic devices before going to bed at night

questions.question: ---- whether the new policy will actually lead to a reduction in carbon emissions.

options.A: It remains to be seen
options.B: The government has announced
options.C: Many experts are convinced
options.D: There is no doubt
options.E: The data clearly shows

questions.question: In light of the recent findings regarding microplastics, ----.

options.A: many countries are considering stricter regulations on single-use plastics
options.B: the ocean is estimated to contain millions of tons of plastic waste
options.C: marine life is severely affected by the ingestion of synthetic fibers
options.D: recycling programs are being expanded to include more types of materials
options.E: consumers are encouraged to use reusable bags and bottles

questions.question: ---- so long as they adhere to the safety guidelines established by the laboratory.

options.A: Students are permitted to conduct their own chemistry experiments
options.B: The research project must be completed by the end of the semester
options.C: The equipment is very expensive and should be handled with care
options.D: Chemical reactions can be dangerous if not monitored properly
options.E: The supervisor is responsible for the well-being of all staff members

questions.question: Artificial intelligence is transforming the job market ----.

options.A: by automating routine tasks while creating new roles in tech-related fields
options.B: even though many people are afraid of losing their jobs to robots
options.C: because it can process vast amounts of data much faster than humans
options.D: so that businesses can improve their efficiency and productivity
options.E: since it was first developed in the mid-twentieth century

questions.question: ---- whereas the northern part of the country is mountainous and cold.

options.A: The southern coast enjoys a Mediterranean climate with warm summers
options.B: The capital city is located in the heart of the industrial region
options.C: Agriculture is the main source of income for the local population
options.D: Many tourists visit the area for its beautiful beaches and resorts
options.E: The railway system connects all the major cities in the country

questions.question: The discovery of the Higgs boson was a major milestone in physics ----.

options.A: as it confirmed the existence of a field that gives mass to particles
options.B: because it took decades of research and billions of dollars to build the LHC
options.C: even if some scientists were skeptical about the initial results
options.D: since it opened up new possibilities for understanding the universe
options.E: so that researchers can now focus on other mysteries of quantum mechanics

questions.question: Unless the infrastructure is significantly upgraded, ----.

options.A: the city will continue to experience frequent power outages and water shortages
options.B: the government has allocated a large budget for public works projects
options.C: many residents have complained about the poor condition of the roads
options.D: the population growth has put a lot of pressure on existing services
options.E: new technologies can be used to improve the efficiency of the grid

questions.question: ---- but the evidence supporting this claim is still inconclusive.

options.A: Some historians believe that the ancient city was destroyed by a flood
options.B: The ruins were discovered by a team of archaeologists last summer
options.C: Many artifacts were found at the site, including pottery and tools
options.D: The excavation process has been delayed due to lack of funding
options.E: Local myths tell the story of a great king who once ruled the land

questions.question: Genetic engineering has the potential to cure many hereditary diseases, ----.

options.A: yet it also raises significant ethical concerns regarding 'designer babies'
options.B: it has been used to create drought-resistant crops in agriculture
options.C: scientists are working on developing new gene-editing techniques
options.D: many patients are waiting for the results of the latest clinical trials
options.E: the cost of such treatments is currently beyond the reach of most people

questions.question: ---- in order to protect the privacy of the participants in the study.

options.A: The researchers used pseudonyms instead of real names in their report
options.B: The data was collected through online surveys and interviews
options.C: All participants were required to sign a consent form before the study
options.D: The findings were presented at an international conference last week
options.E: The study focused on the impact of social media on mental health

questions.question: Although the initial investment was quite high, ----.

options.A: the long-term benefits of the project far outweigh the costs
options.B: the company had to borrow money from several different banks
options.C: many shareholders were worried about the financial risk involved
options.D: the project was completed ahead of schedule and under budget
options.E: the return on investment has been disappointing so far

questions.question: ---- because the current legal framework is no longer sufficient for the digital era.

options.A: Lawmakers are drafting new regulations to govern online commerce
options.B: Cybercrime has become a major threat to national security
options.C: Many people are concerned about the misuse of their personal data
options.D: The internet has changed the way we communicate and do business
options.E: Legal experts are debating the implications of artificial intelligence

questions.question: The rise of remote work has led many people to move away from expensive cities, ----.

options.A: thereby revitalizing smaller towns and rural communities
options.B: since they no longer need to commute to an office every day
options.C: even though some companies are now requiring employees to return
options.D: as high-speed internet becomes more widely available in remote areas
options.E: which has resulted in a decrease in the demand for commercial real estate

questions.question: ---- provided that the patient follows the prescribed post-operative care.

options.A: The recovery process is expected to be quick and without complications
options.B: The surgery was performed by a team of highly skilled surgeons
options.C: Many patients experience some pain and discomfort after the procedure
options.D: The hospital provides excellent facilities for rehabilitation
options.E: A healthy diet and regular exercise are essential for long-term health

questions.question: While some argue that globalization leads to cultural homogenization, ----.

options.A: others believe it promotes cultural exchange and diversity
options.B: it has undoubtedly contributed to the growth of international trade
options.C: many traditional crafts are being lost due to mass production
options.D: global brands are now available in almost every country in the world
options.E: English has become the lingua franca of business and science

questions.question: ---- as if the entire world were watching her every move.

options.A: The young gymnast felt an immense pressure during the final routine
options.B: She had been practicing for several years to reach this level
options.C: The Olympic Games attract millions of viewers from around the globe
options.D: Her performance was praised by both the judges and the audience
options.E: Winning a gold medal has always been her greatest dream

questions.question: Since the discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming, ----.

options.A: the field of medicine has been revolutionized by the use of antibiotics
options.B: many bacterial infections that were once fatal can now be treated
options.C: he was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1945
options.D: other types of antibiotics have been developed to target different bacteria
options.E: the problem of antibiotic resistance has become a major concern

questions.question: No matter how much technology advances, ----.

options.A: the need for human creativity and critical thinking will remain essential
options.B: it will always be impossible to predict the future with total accuracy
options.C: many people will still prefer the personal touch in service industries
options.D: artificial intelligence will never be able to fully replicate human emotions
options.E: the ethical dilemmas posed by new inventions will continue to arise

questions.question: ---- so that the delicate artifacts could be transported safely to the museum.

options.A: The team used specialized packing materials and climate-controlled containers
options.B: The exhibition will feature items from several different ancient civilizations
options.C: Many of the objects were found during a recent excavation in Egypt
options.D: The museum curator is responsible for the preservation of the collection
options.E: Archaeologists were careful not to damage the site during the dig

questions.question: The government has decided to increase funding for public schools ----.

options.A: in order to reduce the gap in educational outcomes between different regions
options.B: because the current budget was not enough to cover the rising costs
options.C: since education is the most important investment for the future of the country
options.D: although some critics argue that the money should be spent elsewhere
options.E: as more and more families are choosing private education for their children

questions.question: ____ the initial results of the study were inconclusive, the team decided to extend the research period by another six months.

options.A: Because
options.B: Although
options.C: Provided that
options.D: In case
options.E: Unless

questions.question: Many historians argue that the industrial revolution was not a sudden event ____ a gradual process spanning several decades.

options.A: rather than
options.B: but rather
options.C: as well as
options.D: in addition to
options.E: so as to

questions.question: The success of the mission depends on the seamless integration of various departments, ____ any miscommunication could lead to failure.

options.A: inasmuch as
options.B: nonetheless
options.C: otherwise
options.D: so that
options.E: whereby

questions.question: ____ advancements in artificial intelligence are undeniable, ethical concerns regarding data privacy remain a major hurdle.

options.A: While
options.B: Since
options.C: Because
options.D: As long as
options.E: If
